To set up your database, you need to run the following SQL code in your Supabase project.

This script will:
1.  Create the `bookings` table with columns matching your `AppContext.tsx` logic.
2.  Enable Row Level Security (RLS) to protect your data.
3.  Create Policies to ensure:
    *   Admins can see all bookings.
    *   Regular users can only see their own bookings.
    *   Anyone (authenticated or anonymous) can create a booking.

### **How to run this:**
1.  Go to your [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard).
2.  Open your project.
3.  Click on **SQL Editor** in the left sidebar.
4.  Paste the code below into the editor and click **Run**.

```sql
-- 1. Create the bookings table
create table public.bookings (
  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now(),
  user_id uuid references auth.users(id), -- Links to the logged-in user
  customer_name text not null,
  customer_phone text not null,
  customer_address text not null,
  booking_type text not null, -- 'SERVICE', 'AMC', or 'PURCHASE'
  service_id text,
  plan_id text,
  purchase_details jsonb, -- Stores the JSON object for AC purchase details
  date text,
  time text,
  status text default 'Pending', -- 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Cancelled'
  ac_type text,
  notes text
);

-- 2. Enable Row Level Security (RLS)
-- This locks the table down so only specific policies allow access
alter table public.bookings enable row level security;

-- 3. Create Policies

-- Policy: Allow users to view their own bookings
create policy "Users can view their own bookings"
on public.bookings for select
using (
  auth.uid() = user_id
);

-- Policy: Allow Admins to view ALL bookings
-- This checks if the user's metadata contains role: 'ADMIN'
create policy "Admins can view all bookings"
on public.bookings for select
using (
  (auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'role') = 'ADMIN'
);

-- Policy: Allow Admins to update bookings (e.g., change status)
create policy "Admins can update bookings"
on public.bookings for update
using (
```
